io.Discard\n194\t\th.SimulatorCmd.Stderr = io.Discard\n195\t\n196\t\tif err := h.SimulatorCmd.Start(); err != nil {\n197\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to start simulator: %w\", /api/nodes\n206\tfunc (h *TestHarness) GetNodes(ctx context.Context) ([]Node, error) {\n207\t\tresp, err := http.Get(h.APIURL + \"/api/nodes\")\n208\t\tif err != nil {\n209\t\t\treturn nil, err\n210\t\t}\n211\t\tdefer resp.Body.Close() //nolint:errcheck\n212\t\n213\t\tvar nodes []NodeRecord\n214\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&nodes); err != nil { //nolint:errcheck\n215\t\t\treturn nil, err\n216\t\t}\n217\t\n218\t\t// Convert NodeRecord to test Node format\n219\t\tresult := make([]Node, 0, len(nodes))\n220\t\tnow := time.Now()\n221\t\tfor _, n := range nodes {\n222\t\t\t// Determine if node is online: seen within last 30 seconds\n223\t\t\tisOnline := now.Sub(n.LastSeenAt) < 30*time.Second\n224\t\t\tresult = append(result, Node{\n225\t\t\t\tMAC:      n.MAC,\n226\t\t\t\tName:     n.Name,\n227\t\t\t\tRole:     n.Role,\n228\t\t\t\tStatus:   map[bool]string{true: \"online\", false: \"offline\"}[isOnline],\n229\t\t\t\tRSSI:     -60, // Not included in NodeRecord response\n230\t\t\t\tUptimeS:  int64(now.Sub(n.FirstSeenAt).Seconds()),\n231\t\t\t\tLastSeen: n.LastSeenAt.UnixMilli(),\n232\t\t\t})\n233\t\t}\n234\t\n235\t\treturn result, nil\n236\t}\n237\t\n238\t// NodeRecord represents a node from the /api/nodes response\n239\ttype NodeRecord struct {\n240\t\tMAC             string    `json:\"mac\"`\n241\t\tName            string    `json:\"name\"`\n242\t\tRole            string    `json:\"role\"`\n243\t\tPosX            float64   `json:\"pos_x\"`\n244\t\tPosY            float64   `json:\"pos_y\"`\n245\t\tPosZ            float64   `json:\"pos_z\"`\n246\t\tVirtual         bool      `json:\"virtual\"`\n247\t\tFirstSeenAt     time.Time `json:\"first_seen_at\"`\n248\t\tLastSeenAt      time.Time `json:\"last_seen_at\"`\n249\t\tFirmwareVersion string    `json:\"firmware_version\"`\n250\t\tChipModel       string    `json:\"chip_model\"`\n251\t\tHealthScore     float64   `json:\"health_score\"`\n252\t}\n253\t\n254\t// Node represents a node from the API (for compatibility with tests)\n255\ttype Node struct {\n256\t\tMAC             string   `json:\"mac\"`\n257\t\tName            string   `json:\"name\"`\n258\t\tRole            string   `json:\"role\"`\n259\t\tPosition        Position `json:\"position\"`\n260\t\tFirmwareVersion string   `json:\"firmware_version\"`\n261\t\tStatus          string   `json:\"status\"`\n262\t\tRSSI            int      `json:\"rssi\"`\n263\t\tUptimeS         int64    `json:\"uptime_s\"`\n264\t\tLastSeen        int64    `json:\"last_seen_ms\"`\n265\t\tPosX            float64  `json:\"pos_x\"`\n266\t\tPosY            float64  `json:\"pos_y\"`\n267\t\tPosZ            float64  `json:\"pos_z\"`\n268\t}\n269\t\n270\t// Position represents a node position\n271\ttype Position struct {\n272\t\tX float64 `json:\"x\"`\n273\t\tY float64 `json:\"y\"`\n274\t\tZ float64 `json:\"z\"`\n275\t}\n276\t\n277\t// GetEvents retrieves events from the API\n278\tfunc (h *TestHarness) GetEvents(ctx context.Context, eventType string, limit int) (*EventsResponse, error) {\n279\t\turl := h.APIURL + \"/api/events\"\n280\t\tif eventType != \"\" {\n281\t\t\turl += \"?type=\" + eventType\n282\t\t}\n283\t\tif limit > 0 {\n284\t\t\tif eventType != \"\" {\n285\t\t\t\turl += \"&\"\n286\t\t\t} else {\n287\t\t\t\turl += \"?\"\n288\t\t\t}\n289\t\t\turl += fmt.Sprintf(\"limit=%d\", limit)\n290\t\t}\n291\t\n292\t\tresp, err := http.Get(url)\n293\t\tif err != nil {\n294\t\t\treturn nil, err\n295\t\t}\n296\t\tdefer resp.Body.Close() //nolint:errcheck\n297\t\n298\t\tvar events EventsResponse\n299\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&events); err != nil { //nolint:errcheck\n300\t\t\treturn nil, err\n301\t\t}\n302\t\n303\t\treturn &events, nil\n304\t}\n305\t\n306\t// EventsResponse represents the /api/events response\n307\ttype EventsResponse struct {\n308\t\tEvents []Event `json:\"events\"`\n309\t\tCursor string  `json:\"cursor,omitempty\"`\n310\t\tTotal  int     `json:\"total,omitempty\"`\n311\t}\n312\t\n313\t// Event represents a single event\n314\ttype Event struct {\n315\t\tID          int64           `json:\"id\"`\n316\t\tTimestampMS int64           `json:\"timestamp_ms\"`\n317\t\tType        string          `json:\"type\"`\n318\t\tZone        string          `json:\"zone,omitempty\"`\n319\t\tPerson      string          `json:\"person,omitempty\"`\n320\t\tBlobID      int             `json:\"blob_id,omitempty\"`\n321\t\tDetail      json.RawMessage `json:\"detail_json,omitempty\"`\n322\t\tSeverity    string          `json:\"severity\"`\n323\t}\n324\t\n325\t// GetBlobCount retrieves the current number of tracked blobs from /api/blobs.\n326\t// The endpoint returns a JSON array of blobs; this helper returns len of that array.\n327\t// It mirrors the GetNodes/GetEvents pattern and is used by AssertDuringRun to\n328\t// observe blob production across a run window.\n329\tfunc (h *TestHarness) GetBlobCount(ctx context.Context) (int, error) {\n330\t\treq, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, h.APIURL+\"/api/blobs\", nil)\n331\t\tif err != nil {\n332\t\t\treturn 0, err\n333\t\t}\n334\t\tresp, err := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)\n335\t\tif err != nil {\n336\t\t\treturn 0, err\n337\t\t}\n338\t\tdefer resp.Body.Close() //nolint:errcheck\n339\t\n340\t\t// /api/blobs serializes the tracked-blob slice directly as a JSON array.\n341\t\tvar blobs []json.RawMessage\n342\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&blobs); err != nil { //nolint:errcheck\n343\t\t\treturn 0, err\n344\t\t}\n345\t\n346\t\treturn len(blobs), nil\n347\t}\n348\t\n349\t// AssertBlobObserved asserts that the dashboard WebSocket feed showed at least one\n350\t// tracked blob at some point. blobCounts is the per-tick slice returned by\n351\t// WatchDashboardWS. Reusable across TestDetectionEvents / TestFullE2EIntegration /\n352\t// the IO-6 hard-gate test (children of bf-5jeo).\n353\tfunc AssertBlobObserved(blobCounts []int) error {\n354\t\tmaxBlobs := 0\n355\t\tfor _, c := range blobCounts {\n356\t\t\tif c > maxBlobs {\n357\t\t\t\tmaxBlobs = c\n358\t\t\t}\n359\t\t}\n360\t\tif maxBlobs < 1 {\n361\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"expected at least one blob in dashboard feed, but max blob count observed was %d across %d ticks\",\n362\t\t\t\tmaxBlobs, len(blobCounts))\n363\t\t}\n364\t\treturn nil\n365\t}\n366\t\n367\t// AssertDetectionEventsObserved asserts that a GetEvents(ctx, \"detection\", N) result\n368\t// contains at least one detection event. Reusable across the e2e detection tests\n369\t// (children of bf-5jeo).\n370\tfunc AssertDetectionEventsObserved(events *EventsResponse) error {\n371\t\tif events == nil {\n372\t\t\treturn errors.New(\"expected at least one detection event, but events response was nil\")\n373\t\t}\n374\t\tif len(events.Events) < 1 {\n375\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"expected at least one detection event, but got %d\", len(events.Events))\n376\t\t}\n377\t\treturn nil\n378\t}\n379\t\n380\t// WatchDashboardWS connects to the dashboard WebSocket and returns blob counts\n381\tfunc (h *TestHarness) WatchDashboardWS(ctx context.Context, duration time.Duration) ([]int, error) {\n382\t\twsURL := \"ws://localhost:8080/ws/dashboard\"\n383\t\tconn, _, err := websocket.DefaultDialer.Dial(wsURL, nil)\n384\t\tif err != nil {\n385\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to connect to dashboard WS: %w\", err)\n386\t\t}\n387\t\tdefer conn.Close() //nolint:errcheck\n388\t\n389\t\tblobCounts := make([]int, 0)\n390\t\tticker := time.NewTicker(1 * time.Second)\n391\t\tdefer ticker.Stop()\n392\t\n393\t\tstartTime := time.Now()\n394\t\n395\t\tfor time.Since(startTime) < duration {\n396\t\t\tselect {\n397\t\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n398\t\t\t\treturn blobCounts, ctx.Err()\n399\t\t\tcase <-ticker.C:\n400\t\t\t\t// Read message with timeout\n401\t\t\t\tconn.SetReadDeadline(time.Now().Add(2 * time.Second))\n402\t\t\t\t_, message, err := conn.ReadMessage()\n403\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n404\t\t\t\t\tif isTimeoutErr(err) || err.Error() == \"EOF\" {\n405\t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n406\t\t\t\t\t}\n407\t\t\t\t\treturn blobCounts, fmt.Errorf(\"read error: %w\", err)\n408\t\t\t\t}\n409\t\n410\t\t\t\t// Parse message\n411\t\t\t\tvar data map[string]interface{}\n412\t\t\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(message, &data); err != nil {\n413\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n414\t\t\t\t}\n415\t\n416\t\t\t\t// Check for blobs in snapshot or delta messages\n417\t\t\t\tblobCount := 0\n418\t\t\t\tif blobs, ok := data[\"blobs\"].([]interface{}); ok {\n419\t\t\t\t\tblobCount = len(blobs)\n420\t\t\t\t}\n421\t\n422\t\t\t\tblobCounts = append(blobCounts, blobCount)\n423\t\t\t}\n424\t\t}\n425\t\n426\t\treturn blobCounts, nil\n427\t}\n428\t\n429\t// AssertDuringRun polls assertions during the simulation run\n430\tfunc (h *TestHarness) AssertDuringRun(ctx context.Context, duration time.Duration, expectedNodes int) error {\n431\t\tticker := time.NewTicker(1 * time.Second)\n432\t\tdefer ticker.Stop()\n433\t\n434\t\tstartTime := time.Now()\n435\t\tnodesSeenOnline := false\n436\t\t// Track blob/detection production across the WHOLE run window so we can\n437\t\t// hard-fail (below) if the fusion pipeline produced no output at all.\n438\t\tmaxBlobs := 0              // peak concurrent tracked blobs seen via /api/blobs\n439\t\tdetectionEventCount := 0  // peak detection-event count seen via /api/events\n440\t\n441\t\tfor time.Since(startTime) < duration {\n442\t\t\tselect {\n443\t\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n444\t\t\t\treturn ctx.Err()\n445\t\t\tcase <-ticker.C:\n446\t\t\t\telapsed := int(time.Since(startTime).Seconds())\n447\t\n448\t\t\t\t// Check health - assert status=='ok' throughout entire run\n449\t\t\t\tresp, err := http.Get(h.APIURL + \"/healthz\")\n450\t\t\t\tif err == nil {\n451\t\t\t\t\tdefer resp.Body.Close() //nolint:errcheck\n452\t\t\t\t\tvar health HealthResponse\n453\t\t\t\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&health); err == nil { //nolint:errcheck\n454\t\t\t\t\t\tif health.Status != \"ok\" {\n455\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"health check failed at %ds: status=%s\", elapsed, health.Status)\n456\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n457\t\t\t\t\t}\n458\t\t\t\t}\n459\t\n460\t\t\t\t// Check nodes - assert nodes_online == expectedNodes within first 5s\n461\t\t\t\tif elapsed <= 5 && !nodesSeenOnline {\n462\t\t\t\t\tnodes, err := h.GetNodes(ctx)\n463\t\t\t\t\tif err == nil {\n464\t\t\t\t\t\tonlineCount := 0\n465\t\t\t\t\t\tfor _, node := range nodes {\n466\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif node.Status == \"online\" {\n467\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tonlineCount++\n468\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n469\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n470\t\t\t\t\t\tif onlineCount >= expectedNodes {\n471\t\t\t\t\t\t\th.t.Logf(\"✓ All %d nodes online within first 5s (elapsed: %ds)\", expectedNodes, elapsed)\n472\t\t\t\t\t\t\tnodesSeenOnline = true\n473\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n474\t\t\t\t\t}\n475\t\t\t\t}\n476\t\n477\t\t\t\t// Track blob production across the whole run window (not just an\n478\t\t\t\t// early sub-window). Polling /api/blobs gives the current concurrent\n479\t\t\t\t// tracked-blob count; we keep the peak.\n480\t\t\t\tif elapsed >= 5 {\n481\t\t\t\t\tif blobCount, err := h.GetBlobCount(ctx); err == nil && blobCount > maxBlobs {\n482\t\t\t\t\t\tmaxBlobs = blobCount\n483\t\t\t\t\t}\n484\t\t\t\t}\n485\t\n486\t\t\t\t// Track detection events across the whole run window. Detection events\n487\t\t\t\t// are emitted only when the fusion+tracking pipeline produces blobs, so\n488\t\t\t\t// they are an independent confirmation of blob production.\n489\t\t\t\tif elapsed >= 5 {\n490\t\t\t\t\tif events, err := h.GetEvents(ctx, \"detection\", 100); err == nil {\n491\t\t\t\t\t\tif n := len(events.Events); n > detectionEventCount {\n492\t\t\t\t\t\t\tdetectionEventCount = n\n493\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n494\t\t\t\t\t}\n495\t\t\t\t}\n496\t\t\t}\n497\t\t}\n498\t\n499\t\t// Hard-fail: across the entire run window the fusion pipeline produced\n500\t\t// neither a tracked blob nor a detection event. This is a regression in\n501\t\t// detection, not a tolerated signal condition — so surface a descriptive\n502\t\t// error (elapsed time + counts observed) rather than silently returning nil.\n503\t\telapsedTotal := int(time.Since(startTime).Seconds())\n504\t\tif maxBlobs < 1 && detectionEventCount < 1 {\n505\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"no blobs or detection events observed during %ds run window \"+\n506\t\t\t\t\"(max concurrent blobs: %d, detection events: %d): fusion pipeline produced no output\",\n507\t\t\t\telapsedTotal, maxBlobs, detectionEventCount)\n508\t\t}\n509\t\n510\t\tif maxBlobs >= 1 {\n511\t\t\th.t.Logf(\"✓ Blobs observed during run (peak concurrent: %d)\", maxBlobs)\n512\t\t}\n513\t\tif detectionEventCount >= 1 {\n514\t\t\th.t.Logf(\"✓ Detection events observed during run (%d events)\", detectionEventCount)\n515\t\t}\n516\t\n517\t\treturn nil\n518\t}\n519\t\n520\t// SimulateNode simulates a single node connection\n521\tfunc (h *TestHarness) SimulateNode(ctx context.Context, mac string, duration time.Duration) error {\n522\t\tconn, _, err := websocket.DefaultDialer.Dial(h.MothershipURL, nil)\n523\t\tif err != nil {\n524\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to connect: %w\", err)\n525\t\t}\n526\t\tdefer conn.Close() //nolint:errcheck\n527\t\n528\t\t// Send hello message\n529\t\thello := map[string]interface{}{\n530\t\t\t\"type\":             \"hello\",\n531\t\t\t\"mac\":              mac,\n532\t\t\t\"node_id\":          \"sim-node-\" + mac,\n533\t\t\t\"firmware_version\": \"0.1.0-sim\",\n534\t\t\t\"capabilities\":     []string{\"csi\", \"tx\", \"rx\"},\n535\t\t\t\"chip\":             \"ESP32-S3\",\n536\t\t\t\"flash_mb\":         16,\n537\t\t\t\"uptime_ms\":        1000,\n538\t\t}\n539\t\n540\t\tif err := conn.WriteJSON(hello); err != nil {\n541\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to send hello: %w\", err)\n542\t\t}\n543\t\n544\t\t// Wait for role assignment\n545\t\ttime.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)\n546\t\n547\t\t// Send CSI frames\n548\t\tticker := time.NewTicker(time.Second / 20) // 20 Hz\n549\t\tdefer ticker.Stop()\n550\t\n551\t\thealthTicker := time.NewTicker(10 * time.Second)\n552\t\tdefer healthTicker.Stop()\n553\t\n554\t\tstartTime := time.Now()\n555\t\tframeIndex := uint64(0)\n556\t\n557\t\tfor time.Since(startTime) < duration {\n558\t\t\tselect {\n559\t\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n560\t\t\t\treturn ctx.Err()\n561\t\t\tcase <-ticker.C:\n562\t\t\t\t// Send CSI frame\n563\t\t\t\tframe := generateCSIFrame(mac, frameIndex)\n564\t\t\t\tif err := conn.WriteMessage(websocket.BinaryMessage, frame); err != nil {\n565\t\t\t\t\t// Tolerate connection close errors near the end of the duration\n566\t\t\t\t\t// (server may have closed the connection gracefully)\n567\t\t\t\t\tif time.Since(startTime) >= duration-500*time.Millisecond {\n568\t\t\t\t\t\treturn nil\n569\t\t\t\t\t}\n570\t\t\t\t\treturn err\n571\t\t\t\t}\n572\t\t\t\tframeIndex++\n573\t\n574\t\t\tcase <-healthTicker.C:\n575\t\t\t\t// Send health message\n576\t\t\t\thealth := map[string]interface{}{\n577\t\t\t\t\t\"type\":            \"health\",\n578\t\t\t\t\t\"mac\":             mac,\n579\t\t\t\t\t\"timestamp_ms\":    time.Now().UnixMilli(),\n580\t\t\t\t\t\"free_heap_bytes\": 204800,\n581\t\t\t\t\t\"wifi_rssi_dbm\":   -60,\n582\t\t\t\t\t\"uptime_ms\":       time.Since(startTime).Milliseconds(),\n583\t\t\t\t\t\"temperature_c\":   42.0,\n584\t\t\t\t\t\"csi_rate_hz\":     20,\n585\t\t\t\t\t\"wifi_channel\":    6,\n586\t\t\t\t}\n587\t\t\t\tif err := conn.WriteJSON(health); err != nil {\n588\t\t\t\t\tif time.Since(startTime) >= duration-500*time.Millisecond {\n589\t\t\t\t\t\treturn nil\n590\t\t\t\t\t}\n591\t\t\t\t\treturn err\n592\t\t\t\t}\n593\t\t\t}\n594\t\n595\t\t\t// Check for reject message\n596\t\t\tconn.SetReadDeadline(time.Now().Add(100 * time.Millisecond))\n597\t\t\t_, msg, err := conn.ReadMessage()\n598\t\t\tif err != nil {\n599\t\t\t\tif !isTimeoutErr(err) {\n600\t\t\t\t\t// Tolerate close errors near end of duration\n601\t\t\t\t\tif time.Since(startTime) >= duration-500*time.Millisecond {\n602\t\t\t\t\t\treturn nil\n603\t\t\t\t\t}\n604\t\t\t\t\treturn err\n605\t\t\t\t}\n606\t\t\t} else if len(msg) > 0 && msg[0] == '{' {\n607\t\t\t\tvar base struct {\n608\t\t\t\t\tType string `json:\"type\"`\n609\t\t\t\t}\n610\t\t\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(msg, &base); err == nil && base.Type == \"reject\" {\n611\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"node rejected\")\n612\t\t\t\t}\n613\t\t\t}\n614\t\t}\n615\t\n616\t\treturn nil\n617\t}\n618\t\n619\t// generateCSIFrame creates a synthetic CSI frame\n620\tfunc generateCSIFrame(mac string, frameIndex uint64) []byte {\n621\t\tconst (\n622\t\t\tHeaderSize  = 24\n623\t\t\tDefaultNSub = 52\n624\t\t)\n625\t\n626\t\tbuf := make([]byte, HeaderSize+DefaultNSub*2)\n627\t\n628\t\t// Parse MAC to bytes\n629\t\tvar macBytes [6]byte\n630\t\tfmt.Sscanf(mac, \"%02X:%02X:%02X:%02X:%02X:%02X\",\n631\t\t\t&macBytes[0], &macBytes[1], &macBytes[2], &macBytes[3], &macBytes[4], &macBytes[5])\n632\t\n633\t\t// Node MAC\n634\t\tcopy(buf[0:6], macBytes[:])\n635\t\n636\t\t// Peer MAC (use a fake peer)\n637\t\tpeerMAC := [6]byte{0x11, 0x22, 0x33, 0x44, 0x55, 0x00}\n638\t\tcopy(buf[6:12], peerMAC[:])\n639\t\n640\t\t// Timestamp\n641\t\ttimestampUS := frameIndex * 50000 // 20 Hz = 50ms\n642\t\tbuf[12] = byte(timestampUS)\n643\t\tbuf[13] = byte(timestampUS >> 8)\n644\t\tbuf[14] = byte(timestampUS >> 16)\n645\t\tbuf[15] = byte(timestampUS >> 24)\n646\t\tbuf[16] = byte(timestampUS >> 32)\n647\t\tbuf[17] = byte(timestampUS >> 40)\n648\t\tbuf[18] = byte(timestampUS >> 48)\n649\t\tbuf[19] = byte(timestampUS >> 56)\n650\t\n651\t\t// RSSI\n652\t\tbuf[20] = 0xdc // -40 dBm\n653\t\n654\t\t// Noise floor\n655\t\tbuf[21] = 0xa1 // -95 dBm\n656\t\n657\t\t// Channel\n658\t\tbuf[22] = 6\n659\t\n660\t\t// Number of subcarriers\n661\t\tbuf[23] = DefaultNSub\n662\t\n663\t\t// Generate CSI payload (I, Q pairs)\n664\t\tfor k := 0; k < DefaultNSub; k++ {\n665\t\t\tamplitude := 30.0 + float64(k)*0.1\n666\t\t\tiVal := int8(amplitude * 0.707) // cos(45deg) ~= 0.707\n667\t\t\tqVal := int8(amplitude * 0.707)\n668\t\n669\t\t\toffset := HeaderSize + k*2\n670\t\t\tbuf[offset] = byte(iVal)\n671\t\t\tbuf[offset+1] = byte(qVal)\n672\t\t}\n673\t\n674\t\treturn buf\n675\t}\n676\t\n677\t// TestMothershipHealth tests that the mothership starts and becomes healthy\n678\tfunc TestMothershipHealth(t *testing.T) {\n679\t\tif testing.Short() {\n680\t\t\tt.Skip(\"skipping e2e test in short mode\")\n681\t\t}\n682\t\n683\t\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), TestTimeout)\n684\t\tdefer cancel()\n685\t\n686\t\th := NewTestHarness(t)\n687\t\tdefer h.Stop()\n688\t\n689\t\tif err := h.Start(ctx); err != nil {\n690\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to start mothership: %v\", err)\n691\t\t}\n692\t\n693\t\t// Check health endpoint\n694\t\tresp, err := http.Get(h.APIURL + \"/healthz\")\n695\t\tif err != nil {\n696\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to get health: %v\", err)\n697\t\t}\n698\t\tdefer resp.Body.Close() //nolint:errcheck\n699\t\n700\t\tvar health HealthResponse\n701\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&health); err != nil { //nolint:errcheck\n702\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to decode health: %v\", err)\n703\t\t}\n704\t\n705\t\tif health.Status != \"ok\" {\n706\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected status ok, got %s\", health.Status)\n707\t\t}\n708\t}\n709\t\n710\t// TestSimulatorConnection tests that the simulator can connect\n711\tfunc TestSimulatorConnection(t *testing.T) {\n712\t\tif testing.Short() {\n713\t\t\tt.Skip(\"skipping e2e test in short mode\")\n714\t\t}\n715\t\n716\t\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), TestTimeout)\n717\t\tdefer cancel()\n718\t\n719\t\th := NewTestHarness(t)\n720\t\tdefer h.Stop()\n721\t\n722\t\tif err := h.Start(ctx); err != nil {\n723\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to start mothership: %v\", err)\n724\t\t}\n725\t\n726\t\t// Run simulator for 10 seconds\n727\t\tif err := h.RunSimulator(ctx, 2, 1, 20, 10*time.Second); err != nil {\n728\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to run simulator: %v\", err)\n729\t\t}\n730\t\n731\t\t// Wait a bit for nodes to connect\n732\t\ttime.Sleep(2 * time.Second)\n733\t\n734\t\t// Check nodes are online\n735\t\tnodes, err := h.GetNodes(ctx)\n736\t\tif err != nil {\n737\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to get nodes: %v\", err)\n738\t\t}\n739\t\n740\t\tonlineCount := 0\n741\t\tfor _, node := range nodes {\n742\t\t\tif node.Status == \"online\" {\n743\t\t\t\tonlineCount++\n744\t\t\t}\n745\t\t}\n746\t\n747\t\tif onlineCount < 2 {\n748\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected at least 2 nodes online, got %d\", onlineCount)\n749\t\t}\n750\t\n751\t\tt.Logf(\"Found %d/%d nodes online\", onlineCount, len(nodes))\n752\t}\n753\t\n754\t// TestDetectionEvents asserts that the events API reflects at least one detection\n755\t// event after a 4-node / 2-walker simulation run. A detection event is only emitted\n756\t// when the fusion+tracking loop produces a tracked blob, so this is a real check on\n757\t// the detection pipeline — not merely an API-reachability smoke test.\n758\t//\n759\t// NOTE: this assertion is deliberately strict and is expected to be RED until the\n760\t// upstream fusion SetNodePosition wiring (bf-4q5w) lands, so that an empty\n761\t// detection-event list surfaces as a hard failure rather than silently passing.\n762\t// That strictness is the entire point of the bf-5jeo verification capstone. Do\n763\t// NOT weaken it (e.g. by re-accepting an empty list) to make the test green.\n764\tfunc TestDetectionEvents(t *testing.T) {\n765\t\tif testing.Short() {\n766\t\t\tt.Skip(\"skipping e2e test in short mode\")\n767\t\t}\n768\t\n769\t\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), TestTimeout)\n770\t\tdefer cancel()\n771\t\n772\t\th := NewTestHarness(t)\n773\t\tdefer h.Stop()\n774\t\n775\t\tif err := h.Start(ctx); err != nil {\n776\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to start mothership: %v\", err)\n777\t\t}\n778\t\n779\t\t// Run simulator: 4 nodes, 2 walkers, 20 Hz, 15 s.\n780\t\tduration := 15 * time.Second\n781\t\tif err := h.RunSimulator(ctx, 4, 2, 20, duration); err != nil {\n782\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to run simulator: %v\", err)\n783\t\t}\n784\t\n785\t\t// Wait for simulation to complete (plus a short grace period for any\n786\t\t// in-flight detection events to be persisted to the events table).\n787\t\ttime.Sleep(duration + 2*time.Second)\n788\t\n789\t\t// Fetch detection events. The endpoint must return a valid response.\n790\t\tevents, err := h.GetEvents(ctx, \"detection\", 100)\n791\t\tif err != nil {\n792\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to get events: %v\", err)\n793\t\t}\n794\t\n795\t\t// Hard assertion: at least one detection event must be present. An empty list\n796\t\t// means the fusion+tracking loop produced no blobs — a detection regression,\n797\t\t// not a tolerated quiet-room condition. The helper (bf-2330) gates nil and\n798\t\t// empty; we wrap its error with the count observed and the likely upstream\n799\t\t// cause so a RED failure is immediately actionable.\n800\t\tif assertErr := AssertDetectionEventsObserved(events); assertErr != nil {\n801\t\t\tobserved := 0\n802\t\t\tif events != nil {\n803\t\t\t\tobserved = len(events.Events)\n804\t\t\t}\n805\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected >=1 detection event but observed %d; \"+\n806\t\t\t\t\"the fusion/tracking pipeline produced no blobs (no walker was \"+\n807\t\t\t\t\"localized from the 4-node/2-walker CSI stream) [%v]\",\n808\t\t\t\tobserved, assertErr)\n809\t\t}\n810\t\n811\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Detection events observed: %d\", len(events.Events))\n812\t}\n813\t\n814\t// TestConcurrentNodes tests multiple concurrent node connections\n815\tfunc TestConcurrentNodes(t *testing.T) {\n816\t\tif testing.Short() {\n817\t\t\tt.Skip(\"skipping e2e test in short mode\")\n818\t\t}\n819\t\n820\t\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), TestTimeout)\n821\t\tdefer cancel()\n822\t\n823\t\th := NewTestHarness(t)\n824\t\tdefer h.Stop()\n825\t\n826\t\tif err := h.Start(ctx); err != nil {\n827\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to start mothership: %v\", err)\n828\t\t}\n829\t\n830\t\t// Simulate 4 concurrent nodes\n831\t\tvar wg sync.WaitGroup\n832\t\tnodeMACs := []string{\n833\t\t\t\"AA:BB:CC:DD:00:01\",\n834\t\t\t\"AA:BB:CC:DD:00:02\",\n835\t\t\t\"AA:BB:CC:DD:00:03\",\n836\t\t\t\"AA:BB:CC:DD:00:04\",\n837\t\t}\n838\t\n839\t\tduration := 10 * time.Second\n840\t\tfor _, mac := range nodeMACs {\n841\t\t\twg.Add(1)\n842\t\t\tgo func(mac string) {\n843\t\t\t\tdefer wg.Done()\n844\t\t\t\tif err := h.SimulateNode(ctx, mac, duration); err != nil {\n845\t\t\t\t\t// Log connection errors but don't fail the test here —\n846\t\t\t\t\t// the node count check below is the authoritative assertion.\n847\t\t\t\t\t// Broken pipe / closed connections can happen normally during\n848\t\t\t\t\t// concurrent role rebalancing.\n849\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Node %s connection error (may be normal): %v\", mac, err)\n850\t\t\t\t}\n851\t\t\t}(mac)\n852\t\t}\n853\t\n854\t\twg.Wait()\n855\t\n856\t\t// Check all nodes are online\n857\t\tnodes, err := h.GetNodes(ctx)\n858\t\tif err != nil {\n859\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to get nodes: %v\", err)\n860\t\t}\n861\t\n862\t\tonlineCount := 0\n863\t\tfor _, node := range nodes {\n864\t\t\tif node.Status == \"online\" {\n865\t\t\t\tonlineCount++\n866\t\t\t}\n867\t\t}\n868\t\n869\t\tif onlineCount < 4 {\n870\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected at least 4 nodes online, got %d\", onlineCount)\n871\t\t}\n872\t\n873\t\tt.Logf(\"Successfully connected %d nodes\", onlineCount)\n874\t}\n875\t\n876\t// TestDashboardWebSocket tests the dashboard WebSocket connection\n877\tfunc TestDashboardWebSocket(t *testing.T) {\n878\t\tif testing.Short() {\n879\t\t\tt.Skip(\"skipping e2e test in short mode\")\n880\t\t}\n881\t\n882\t\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), TestTimeout)\n883\t\tdefer cancel()\n884\t\n885\t\th := NewTestHarness(t)\n886\t\tdefer h.Stop()\n887\t\n888\t\tif err := h.Start(ctx); Asserting neither — the\n912\t// previous behavior — let detection regressions pass silently as long as the API\n913\t// was merely reachable. The two hard assertions below close that dodge.\n914\t//\n915\t// NOTE: these assertions are deliberately strict and are expected to be RED until\n916\t// the upstream fusion SetNodePosition wiring (bf-4q5w) lands, so that an empty\n917\t// blob feed / detection-event list surfaces as a hard failure rather than silently\n918\t// passing. That strictness is the entire point of the bf-5jeo capstone. Do NOT\n919\t// weaken it (e.g. by re-accepting an empty feed or list) to make the test green.\n920\tfunc TestFullE2EIntegration(t *testing.T) {\n921\t\tif testing.Short() {\n922\t\t\tt.Skip(\"skipping e2e test in short mode\")\n923\t\t}\n924\t\n925\t\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), TestTimeout)\n926\t\tdefer cancel()\n927\t\n928\t\th := NewTestHarness(t)\n929\t\tdefer h.Stop()\n930\t\n931\t\tif err := h.Start(ctx); err != nil {\n932\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to start mothership: %v\", err)\n933\t\t}\n934\t\n935\t\t// Run simulator with 4 nodes, 2 walkers\n936\t\tsimDuration := 30 * time.Second\n937\t\tif err := h.RunSimulator(ctx, 4, 2, 20, simDuration); err != nil {\n938\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to run simulator: %v\", err)\n939\t\t}\n940\t\n941\t\t// Capture the dashboard WebSocket blob feed concurrently while the\n942\t\t// simulation runs and AssertDuringRun polls the live state. WatchDashboardWS\n943\t\t// blocks for `simDuration`, so it must run alongside AssertDuringRun rather\n944\t\t// than after it (the simulator has finished by then).\n945\t\tvar blobCounts []int\n946\t\tvar wsErr error\n947\t\tvar wsWG sync.WaitGroup\n948\t\twsWG.Add(1)\n949\t\tgo func() {\n950\t\t\tdefer wsWG.Done()\n951\t\t\tblobCounts, wsErr = h.WatchDashboardWS(ctx, simDuration)\n952\t\t}()\n953\t\n954\t\t// Assert during run (kept as-is; AssertDuringRun itself is hardened by bf-2330).\n955\t\tif err := h.AssertDuringRun(ctx, simDuration, 4); err != nil {\n956\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Assertion failed during run: %v\", err)\n957\t\t}\n958\t\n959\t\t// Collect the dashboard WS capture.\n960\t\twsWG.Wait()\n961\t\tif wsErr != nil {\n962\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to watch dashboard WS: %v\", wsErr)\n963\t\t}\n964\t\n965\t\t// Wait for simulator to complete (grace period for any in-flight detection\n966\t\t// events to be persisted to the events table).\n967\t\ttime.Sleep(simDuration + 2*time.Second)\n968\t\n969\t\t// Hard assertion #1: the dashboard WebSocket feed must have shown at least one\n970\t\t// tracked blob during the run. The helper (bf-2330) gates\n973\t\t// nil/empty/zero; we wrap its error with the peak observed and likely upstream\n974\t\t// cause so a RED failure is immediately actionable.\n975\t\tif assertErr := AssertBlobObserved(blobCounts); assertErr != nil {\n976\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected >=1 blob in the dashboard WS feed during the run, \"+\n977\t\t\t\t\"but none was observed (the fusion/tracking pipeline localized no \"+\n978\t\t\t\t\"walker from the 4-node/2-walker CSI stream) [%v]\", assertErr)\n979\t\t}\n980\t\n981\t\t// Hard assertion #2: the /api/events \"detection\" stream must surface at least\n982\t\t// one detection event. Same rationale as above — an empty list is a detection\n983\t\t// regression, not a tolerated condition.\n984\t\tevents, err := h.GetEvents(ctx, \"detection\", 100)\n985\t\tif err != nil {\n986\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to get events: %v\", err)\n987\t\t}\n988\t\tif assertErr := AssertDetectionEventsObserved(events); assertErr != nil {\n989\t\t\tobserved := 0\n990\t\t\tif events != nil {\n991\t\t\t\tobserved = len(events.Events)\n992\t\t\t}\n993\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected >=1 detection event but observed %d; \"+\n994\t\t\t\t\"the fusion/tracking pipeline produced no blobs (no walker was \"+\n995\t\t\t\t\"localized from the 4-node/2-walker CSI stream) [%v]\",\n996\t\t\t\tobserved, assertErr)\n997\t\t}\n998\t\n999\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Full E2E integration test passed (dashboard WS showed >=1 blob; %d detection events)\", len(events.Events))\n1000\t}\n1001\t\n1002\t// IO_1_FreshInstall_FirstBoot tests the fresh install / first boot scenario.\n1003\t// This is a hard-gate test for {\n\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, HealthTimeout)\n\tdefer cancel()\n\n\tticker := time.NewTicker(500 * time.Millisecond)\n\tdefer ticker.Stop()\n\n\tfor {\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n\t\t\treturn ctx.Err()\n\t\tcase <-ticker.C:\n\t\t\tresp, err := http.Get(h.APIURL + \"/healthz\")\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tdefer resp.Body.Close()   string `json:\"db\"`\n\tSheddingLevel int    `json:\"shedding_level\"`\n}\n\n// RunSimulator starts the simulator\nfunc (h *TestHarness) RunSimulator(ctx context.Context, nodes, walkers, rate int, `json:\"total,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// Event represents a single event\ntype Event struct {\n\tID          int64           `json:\"id\"`\n\tTimestampMS int64           `json:\"timestamp_ms\"`\n\tType        string          `json:\"type\"`\n\tZone        string          `json:\"zone,omitempty\"`\n\tPerson      string          `json:\"person,omitempty\"`\n\tBlobID      int tracked blobs from /api/blobs.\n// The endpoint returns a JSON array of blobs; this helper returns len of that array.\n// It mirrors the GetNodes/GetEvents pattern and is used by AssertDuringRun to\n// observe blob production across a run window.\nfunc (h *TestHarness) GetBlobCount(ctx context.Context) (int, error) {\n\treq, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, h.APIURL+\"/api/blobs\", nil)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn 0, err\n\t}\n\tresp, err := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn 0, err\n\t}\n\tdefer resp.Body.Close() //nolint:errcheck\n\n\t// /api/blobs serializes the tracked-blob slice directly as a JSON array.\n\tvar blobs []json.RawMessage\n\tif err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&blobs); err != nil { //nolint:errcheck\n\t\treturn 0, err\n\t}\n\n\treturn len(blobs), nil\n}\n\n// AssertBlobObserved asserts that the dashboard WebSocket feed showed at least one\n// tracked blob at some point. blobCounts is the per-tick slice returned by\n// WatchDashboardWS. Reusable across TestDetectionEvents / TestFullE2EIntegration /\n// the IO-6 hard-gate test (children of bf-5jeo).\nfunc AssertBlobObserved(blobCounts []int) error {\n\tmaxBlobs := 0\n\tfor _, c := range blobCounts {\n\t\tif c > maxBlobs {\n\t\t\tmaxBlobs = c\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif maxBlobs < 1 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"expected at least one blob in dashboard feed, but max blob count observed was %d across %d ticks\",\n\t\t\tmaxBlobs, len(blobCounts))\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// AssertDetectionEventsObserved asserts that a GetEvents(ctx, \"detection\", N) result\n// contains at least one detection event. Reusable across the e2e detection tests\n// (children of bf-5jeo).\nfunc AssertDetectionEventsObserved(events *EventsResponse) error {\n\tif events == nil {\n\t\treturn errors.New(\"expected at least one detection event, but events response was nil\")\n\t}\n\tif len(events.Events) < 1 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"expected at least one detection event, but got %d\", len(events.Events))\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// WatchDashboardWS connects to the dashboard WebSocket and returns blob counts\nfunc (h *TestHarness) WatchDashboardWS(ctx context.Context, duration time.Duration) ([]int, error) {\n\twsURL := \"ws://localhost:8080/ws/dashboard\"\n\tconn, _, err := websocket.DefaultDialer.Dial(wsURL, nil)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to connect to dashboard WS: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer conn.Close() //nolint:errcheck\n\n\tblobCounts := make([]int, 0)\n\tticker := time.NewTicker(1 * time.Second)\n\tdefer ticker.Stop()\n\n\tstartTime := time.Now()\n\n\tfor time.Since(startTime) < duration {\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n\t\t\treturn blobCounts, ctx.Err()\n\t\tcase <-ticker.C:\n\t\t\t// Read message with timeout\n\t\t\tconn.SetReadDeadline(time.Now().Add(2 * time.Second))\n\t\t\t_, message, err := conn.ReadMessage()\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tif isTimeoutErr(err) || err.Error() == \"EOF\" {\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\treturn blobCounts, fmt.Errorf(\"read error: %w\", err)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Parse message\n\t\t\tvar data map[string]interface{}\n\t\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(message, &data); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Check for blobs in snapshot or delta messages\n\t\t\tblobCount := 0\n\t\t\tif blobs, ok := data[\"blobs\"].([]interface{}); ok {\n\t\t\t\tblobCount = len(blobs)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tblobCounts = append(blobCounts, blobCount)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn blobCounts, nil\n}\n\n// AssertDuringRun polls assertions during the simulation run\nfunc (h *TestHarness) AssertDuringRun(ctx context.Context, duration time.Duration, expectedNodes int) error {\n\tticker := time.NewTicker(1 * time.Second)\n\tdefer ticker.Stop()\n\n\tstartTime := time.Now()\n\tnodesSeenOnline := false\n\t// Track blob/detection production across the WHOLE run window so we can\n\t// hard-fail (below) if the fusion pipeline produced no output at all.\n\tmaxBlobs := 0              // peak concurrent tracked blobs seen via /api/blobs\n\tdetectionEventCount := 0  // peak detection-event count seen via /api/events\n\n\tfor time.Since(startTime) < duration {\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n\t\t\treturn ctx.Err()\n\t\tcase <-ticker.C:\n\t\t\telapsed := int(time.Since(startTime).Seconds())\n\n\t\t\t// Check health - assert status=='ok' throughout entire run\n\t\t\tresp, err := http.Get(h.APIURL + \"/healthz\")\n\t\t\tif err == nil {\n\t\t\t\tdefer resp.Body.Close() //nolint:errcheck\n\t\t\t\tvar health HealthResponse\n\t\t\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&health); err == nil { //nolint:errcheck\n\t\t\t\t\tif health.Status != \"ok\" {\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"health check failed at %ds: status=%s\", elapsed, health.Status)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Check nodes - assert nodes_online == expectedNodes within first 5s\n\t\t\tif elapsed <= 5 && !nodesSeenOnline {\n\t\t\t\tnodes, err := h.GetNodes(ctx)\n\t\t\t\tif err == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tonlineCount := 0\n\t\t\t\t\tfor _, node := range nodes {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif node.Status == \"online\" {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tonlineCount++\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\tif onlineCount >= expectedNodes {\n\t\t\t\t\t\th.t.Logf(\"✓ All %d nodes online within first 5s (elapsed: %ds)\", expectedNodes, elapsed)\n\t\t\t\t\t\tnodesSeenOnline = true\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Track blob production across the whole run window (not just an\n\t\t\t// early sub-window). Polling /api/blobs gives the current concurrent\n\t\t\t// tracked-blob count; we keep the peak.\n\t\t\tif elapsed >= 5 {\n\t\t\t\tif blobCount, err := h.GetBlobCount(ctx); err == nil && blobCount > maxBlobs {\n\t\t\t\t\tmaxBlobs = blobCount\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Track detection events across the whole run window. Detection events\n\t\t\t// are emitted only when the fusion+tracking pipeline produces blobs, so\n\t\t\t// they are an independent confirmation of blob production.\n\t\t\tif elapsed >= 5 {\n\t\t\t\tif events, err := h.GetEvents(ctx, \"detection\", 100); err == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tif n := len(events.Events); n > detectionEventCount {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tdetectionEventCount = n\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Hard-fail: across the entire run window the fusion pipeline produced\n\t// neither a tracked blob nor a detection event. This is a regression in\n\t// detection, not a tolerated signal condition — so surface a descriptive\n\t// error (elapsed time + counts observed) rather than silently returning nil.\n\telapsedTotal := int(time.Since(startTime).Seconds())\n\tif maxBlobs < 1 && detectionEventCount < 1 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"no blobs or detection events observed during %ds run window \"+\n\t\t\t\"(max concurrent blobs: %d, detection events: %d): fusion pipeline produced no output\",\n\t\t\telapsedTotal, maxBlobs, detectionEventCount)\n\t}\n\n\tif maxBlobs >= 1 {\n\t\th.t.Logf(\"✓ Blobs observed during run (peak concurrent: %d)\", maxBlobs)\n\t}\n\tif detectionEventCount >= 1 {\n\t\th.t.Logf(\"✓ Detection events observed during run (%d events)\", detectionEventCount)\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// SimulateNode simulates a single node connection\nfunc (h *TestHarness) SimulateNode(ctx context.Context, mac string, duration time.Duration) error {\n\tconn, _, err := websocket.DefaultDialer.Dial(h.MothershipURL, nil)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to connect: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer conn.Close() //nolint:errcheck\n\n\t// Send hello message\n\thello := map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"type\":             \"hello\",\n\t\t\"mac\":              mac,\n\t\t\"node_id\":          \"sim-node-\" + mac,\n\t\t\"firmware_version\": \"0.1.0-sim\",\n\t\t\"capabilities\":     []string{\"csi\", \"tx\", \"rx\"},\n\t\t\"chip\":             \"ESP32-S3\",\n\t\t\"flash_mb\":         16,\n\t\t\"uptime_ms\":        1000,\n\t}\n\n\tif err := conn.WriteJSON(hello); err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to send hello: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Wait for role assignment\n\ttime.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)\n\n\t// Send CSI frames\n\tticker := time.NewTicker(time.Second / 20) // 20 Hz\n\tdefer ticker.Stop()\n\n\thealthTicker := time.NewTicker(10 * time.Second)\n\tdefer healthTicker.Stop()\n\n\tstartTime := time.Now()\n\tframeIndex := uint64(0)\n\n\tfor time.Since(startTime) < duration {\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n\t\t\treturn ctx.Err()\n\t\tcase <-ticker.C:\n\t\t\t// Send CSI frame\n\t\t\tframe := generateCSIFrame(mac, frameIndex)\n\t\t\tif err := conn.WriteMessage(websocket.BinaryMessage, frame); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t// Tolerate connection close errors near the end of the duration\n\t\t\t\t// (server may have closed the connection gracefully)\n\t\t\t\tif time.Since(startTime) >= duration-500*time.Millisecond {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tframeIndex++\n\n\t\tcase <-healthTicker.C:\n\t\t\t// Send health message\n\t\t\thealth := map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\t\"type\":            \"health\",\n\t\t\t\t\"mac\":             mac,\n\t\t\t\t\"timestamp_ms\":    time.Now().UnixMilli(),\n\t\t\t\t\"free_heap_bytes\": 204800,\n\t\t\t\t\"wifi_rssi_dbm\":   -60,\n\t\t\t\t\"uptime_ms\":       time.Since(startTime).Milliseconds(),\n\t\t\t\t\"temperature_c\":   42.0,\n\t\t\t\t\"csi_rate_hz\":     20,\n\t\t\t\t\"wifi_channel\":    6,\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif err := conn.WriteJSON(health); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tif time.Since(startTime) >= duration-500*time.Millisecond {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check for reject message\n\t\tconn.SetReadDeadline(time.Now().Add(100 * time.Millisecond))\n\t\t_, msg, err := conn.ReadMessage()\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tif !isTimeoutErr(err) {\n\t\t\t\t// Tolerate close errors near end of duration\n\t\t\t\tif time.Since(startTime) >= duration-500*time.Millisecond {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t} else if len(msg) > 0 && msg[0] == '{' {\n\t\t\tvar base struct {\n\t\t\t\tType string `json:\"type\"`\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(msg, &base); err == nil && base.Type == \"reject\" {\n\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"node rejected\")\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// generateCSIFrame creates a synthetic CSI frame\nfunc generateCSIFrame(mac string, frameIndex uint64) []byte {\n\tconst (\n\t\tHeaderSize  = 24\n\t\tDefaultNSub = 52\n\t)\n\n\tbuf := make([]byte, HeaderSize+DefaultNSub*2)\n\n\t// Parse MAC to bytes\n\tvar macBytes [6]byte\n\tfmt.Sscanf(mac, \"%02X:%02X:%02X:%02X:%02X:%02X\",\n\t\t&macBytes[0], &macBytes[1], &macBytes[2], &macBytes[3], &macBytes[4], &macBytes[5])\n\n\t// Node MAC\n\tcopy(buf[0:6], macBytes[:])\n\n\t// Peer MAC (use a fake peer)\n\tpeerMAC := [6]byte{0x11, 0x22, 0x33, 0x44, 0x55, 0x00}\n\tcopy(buf[6:12], peerMAC[:])\n\n\t// Timestamp\n\ttimestampUS := frameIndex * 50000 // 20 Hz = 50ms\n\tbuf[12] = byte(timestampUS)\n\tbuf[13] = byte(timestampUS >> 8)\n\tbuf[14] = byte(timestampUS >> 16)\n\tbuf[15] = byte(timestampUS >> 24)\n\tbuf[16] = byte(timestampUS >> 32)\n\tbuf[17] = byte(timestampUS >> 40)\n\tbuf[18] = byte(timestampUS >> 48)\n\tbuf[19] = byte(timestampUS >> 56)\n\n\t// RSSI\n\tbuf[20] = 0xdc // -40 dBm\n\n\t// Noise floor\n\tbuf[21] = 0xa1 // -95 dBm\n\n\t// Channel\n\tbuf[22] = 6\n\n\t// Number of subcarriers\n\tbuf[23] = DefaultNSub\n\n\t// Generate CSI payload (I, Q pairs)\n\tfor k := 0; k < DefaultNSub; k++ {\n\t\tamplitude := 30.0 + float64(k)*0.1\n\t\tiVal := int8(amplitude * 0.707) // cos(45deg) ~= 0.707\n\t\tqVal := int8(amplitude * 0.707)\n\n\t\toffset := HeaderSize + k*2\n\t\tbuf[offset] = byte(iVal)\n\t\tbuf[offset+1] = byte(qVal)\n\t}\n\n\treturn buf\n}\n\n// TestMothershipHealth tests that the mothership starts and becomes healthy\nfunc TestMothershipHealth(t *testing.T) {\n\tif testing.Short() {\n\t\tt.Skip(\"skipping e2e test in short mode\")\n\t}\n\n\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), TestTimeout)\n\tdefer cancel()\n\n\th := NewTestHarness(t)\n\tdefer h.Stop()\n\n\tif err := h.Start(ctx); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to start mothership: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Check health endpoint\n\tresp, err := http.Get(h.APIURL + \"/healthz\")\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to get health: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer resp.Body.Close() //nolint:errcheck\n\n\tvar health HealthResponse\n\tif err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&health); err != nil { //nolint:errcheck\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to decode health: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tif health.Status != \"ok\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected status ok, got %s\", health.Status)\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestSimulatorConnection tests that the simulator can connect\nfunc TestSimulatorConnection(t *testing.T) {\n\tif testing.Short() {\n\t\tt.Skip(\"skipping e2e test in short mode\")\n\t}\n\n\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), TestTimeout)\n\tdefer cancel()\n\n\th := NewTestHarness(t)\n\tdefer h.Stop()\n\n\tif err := h.Start(ctx); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to start mothership: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Run simulator for 10 seconds\n\tif err := h.RunSimulator(ctx, 2, 1, 20, 10*time.Second); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to run simulator: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Wait a bit for nodes to connect\n\ttime.Sleep(2 * time.Second)\n\n\t// Check nodes are online\n\tnodes, err := h.GetNodes(ctx)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to get nodes: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tonlineCount := 0\n\tfor _, node := range nodes {\n\t\tif node.Status == \"online\" {\n\t\t\tonlineCount++\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif onlineCount < 2 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected at least 2 nodes online, got %d\", onlineCount)\n\t}\n\n\tt.Logf(\"Found %d/%d nodes online\", onlineCount, len(nodes))\n}\n\n// TestDetectionEvents asserts that the events API reflects at least one detection\n// event after a 4-node / 2-walker simulation run. A detection event is only emitted\n// when the fusion+tracking loop produces a tracked blob, so this is a real check on\n// the detection pipeline — not merely an API-reachability smoke test.\n//\n// NOTE: this assertion is deliberately strict and is expected to be RED until the\n// upstream fusion SetNodePosition wiring (bf-4q5w) lands, so that an empty\n// detection-event list surfaces as a hard failure rather than silently passing.\n// That strictness is the entire point of the bf-5jeo verification capstone. Do\n// NOT weaken it (e.g. by re-accepting an empty list) to make the test green.\nfunc TestDetectionEvents(t *testing.T) {\n\tif testing.Short() {\n\t\tt.Skip(\"skipping e2e test in short mode\")\n\t}\n\n\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), TestTimeout)\n\tdefer cancel()\n\n\th := NewTestHarness(t)\n\tdefer h.Stop()\n\n\tif err := h.Start(ctx); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to start mothership: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Run simulator: 4 nodes, 2 walkers, 20 Hz, 15 s.\n\tduration := 15 * time.Second\n\tif err := h.RunSimulator(ctx, 4, 2, 20, duration); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to run simulator: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Wait for simulation to complete (plus a short grace period for any\n\t// in-flight detection events to be persisted to the events table).\n\ttime.Sleep(duration + 2*time.Second)\n\n\t// Fetch detection events. The endpoint must return a valid response.\n\tevents, err := h.GetEvents(ctx, \"detection\", 100)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to get events: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Hard assertion: at least one detection event must be present. An empty list\n\t// means the fusion+tracking loop produced no blobs — a detection regression,\n\t// not a tolerated quiet-room condition. The helper (bf-2330) gates nil and\n\t// empty; we wrap its error with the count observed and the likely upstream\n\t// cause so a RED failure is immediately actionable.\n\tif assertErr := AssertDetectionEventsObserved(events); assertErr != nil {\n\t\tobserved := 0\n\t\tif events != nil {\n\t\t\tobserved = len(events.Events)\n\t\t}\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected >=1 detection event but observed %d; \"+\n\t\t\t\"the fusion/tracking pipeline produced no blobs (no walker was \"+\n\t\t\t\"localized from the 4-node/2-walker CSI stream) [%v]\",\n\t\t\tobserved, assertErr)\n\t}\n\n\tt.Logf(\"✓ Detection events observed: %d\", len(events.Events))\n}\n\n// TestConcurrentNodes tests multiple concurrent node connections\nfunc TestConcurrentNodes(t *testing.T) {\n\tif testing.Short() {\n\t\tt.Skip(\"skipping e2e test in short mode\")\n\t}\n\n\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), TestTimeout)\n\tdefer cancel()\n\n\th := NewTestHarness(t)\n\tdefer h.Stop()\n\n\tif err := h.Start(ctx); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to start mothership: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Simulate 4 concurrent nodes\n\tvar wg sync.WaitGroup\n\tnodeMACs := []string{\n\t\t\"AA:BB:CC:DD:00:01\",\n\t\t\"AA:BB:CC:DD:00:02\",\n\t\t\"AA:BB:CC:DD:00:03\",\n\t\t\"AA:BB:CC:DD:00:04\",\n\t}\n\n\tduration := 10 * time.Second\n\tfor _, mac := range nodeMACs {\n\t\twg.Add(1)\n\t\tgo func(mac string) {\n\t\t\tdefer wg.Done()\n\t\t\tif err := h.SimulateNode(ctx, mac, duration); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t// Log connection errors but don't fail the test here —\n\t\t\t\t// the node count check below is the authoritative assertion.\n\t\t\t\t// Broken pipe / closed connections can happen normally during\n\t\t\t\t// concurrent role rebalancing.\n\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Node %s connection error (may be normal): %v\", mac, err)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}(mac)\n\t}\n\n\twg.Wait()\n\n\t// Check all nodes are online\n\tnodes, err := h.GetNodes(ctx)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to get nodes: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tonlineCount := 0\n\tfor _, node := range nodes {\n\t\tif node.Status == \"online\" {\n\t\t\tonlineCount++\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif onlineCount < 4 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected at least 4 nodes online, got %d\", onlineCount)\n\t}\n\n\tt.Logf(\"Successfully connected %d nodes\", onlineCount)\n}\n\n// TestDashboardWebSocket tests the dashboard WebSocket connection\nfunc TestDashboardWebSocket(t *testing.T) {\n\tif testing.Short() {\n\t\tt.Skip(\"skipping e2e test in short mode\")\n\t}\n\n\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), TestTimeout)\n\tdefer cancel()\n\n\th := NewTestHarness(t)\n\tdefer h.Stop()\n\n\tif err := h.Start(ctx); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to start mothership: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Run simulator for 10 seconds\n\tif err := h.RunSimulator(ctx, 2, 1, 20, 10*time.Second); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to run simulator: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Watch dashboard WebSocket for blob data\n\tblobCounts, err := h.WatchDashboardWS(ctx, 10*time.Second)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to watch dashboard WS: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tt.Logf(\"Received %d blob count updates\", len(blobCounts))\n}\n\n// TestFullE2EIntegration runs a comprehensive end-to-end test.\n//\n// It is the bf-5jeo verification capstone's full-stack check: the 4-node /\n// 2-walker / 30s simulator run must produce observable output on BOTH the\n// dashboard WebSocket feed (a tracked blob appeared) and the /api/events\n// \"detection\" stream (a detection event was persisted). Asserting neither — the\n// previous behavior — let detection regressions pass silently as long as the API\n// was merely reachable. The two hard assertions below close that dodge.\n//\n// NOTE: these assertions are deliberately strict and are expected to be RED until\n// the upstream fusion SetNodePosition wiring (bf-4q5w) lands, so that an empty\n// blob feed / detection-event list surfaces as a hard failure rather than silently\n// passing. That strictness is the entire point of the bf-5jeo capstone. Do NOT\n// weaken it (e.g. by re-accepting an empty feed or list) to make the test green.\nfunc TestFullE2EIntegration(t *testing.T) {\n\tif testing.Short() {\n\t\tt.Skip(\"skipping e2e test in short mode\")\n\t}\n\n\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), TestTimeout)\n\tdefer cancel()\n\n\th := NewTestHarness(t)\n\tdefer h.Stop()\n\n\tif err := h.Start(ctx); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to start mothership: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Run simulator with 4 nodes, 2 walkers\n\tsimDuration := 30 * time.Second\n\tif err := h.RunSimulator(ctx, 4, 2, 20, simDuration); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to run simulator: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Capture the dashboard WebSocket blob feed concurrently while the\n\t// simulation runs and AssertDuringRun polls the live state. WatchDashboardWS\n\t// blocks for `simDuration`, so it must run alongside AssertDuringRun rather\n\t// than after it (the simulator has finished by then).\n\tvar blobCounts []int\n\tvar wsErr error\n\tvar wsWG sync.WaitGroup\n\twsWG.Add(1)\n\tgo func() {\n\t\tdefer wsWG.Done()\n\t\tblobCounts, wsErr = h.WatchDashboardWS(ctx, simDuration)\n\t}()\n\n\t// Assert during run (kept as-is; AssertDuringRun itself is hardened by bf-2330).\n\tif err := h.AssertDuringRun(ctx, simDuration, 4); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Assertion failed during run: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Collect the dashboard WS capture.\n\twsWG.Wait()\n\tif wsErr != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to watch dashboard WS: %v\", wsErr)\n\t}\n\n\t// Wait for simulator to complete (grace period for any in-flight detection\n\t// events to be persisted to the events table).\n\ttime.Sleep(simDuration + 2*time.Second)\n\n\t// Hard assertion #1: the dashboard WebSocket feed must have shown at least one\n\t// tracked blob during the run. A zero blob count means the fusion+tracking\n\t// loop localized no walker from the 4-node/2-walker CSI stream — a detection\n\t// regression, not a tolerated quiet-room condition. The helper (bf-2330) gates\n\t// nil/empty/zero; we wrap its error with the peak observed and likely upstream\n\t// cause so a RED failure is immediately actionable.\n\tif assertErr := AssertBlobObserved(blobCounts); assertErr != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected >=1 blob in the dashboard WS feed during the run, \"+\n\t\t\t\"but none was observed (the fusion/tracking pipeline localized no \"+\n\t\t\t\"walker from the 4-node/2-walker CSI stream) [%v]\", assertErr)\n\t}\n\n\t// Hard assertion #2: the /api/events \"detection\" stream must surface at least\n\t// one detection event. Same rationale as above — an empty list is a detection\n\t// regression, not a tolerated condition.\n\tevents, err := h.GetEvents(ctx, \"detection\", 100)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to get events: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tif assertErr := AssertDetectionEventsObserved(events); assertErr != nil {\n\t\tobserved := 0\n\t\tif events != nil {\n\t\t\tobserved = len(events.Events)\n\t\t}\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected >=1 detection event but observed %d; \"+\n\t\t\t\"the fusion/tracking pipeline produced no blobs (no walker was \"+\n\t\t\t\"localized from the 4-node/2-walker CSI stream) [%v]\",\n\t\t\tobserved, assertErr)\n\t}\n\n\tt.Logf(\"✓ Full E2E integration test passed (dashboard WS showed >=1 blob; %d detection events)\", len(events.Events))\n}\n\n// IO_1_FreshInstall_FirstBoot tests the fresh install / first boot scenario.\n// This is a hard-gate test for releases.\n//\n// Setup: mothership container started with an empty data volume.\n// Steps: GET `/`; complete first-run PIN setup (`POST /api/auth/setup`); poll `/api/health`.\n// Pass: first-run setup page served (200) while no PIN exists; after setup, migrations run\n// (log \"Schema migration applied … All systems ready\"), PIN persists, `/api/health` green,\n// first-run detection now reports `pin_configured: true`; the server reaches ready with **no** node attached.\nfunc IO_1_FreshInstall_FirstBoot(t *testing.T) {\n\tif testing.Short() {\n\t\tt.Skip(\"skipping e2e test in short mode\")\n\t}\n\n\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), TestTimeout)\n\tdefer cancel()\n\n\th := NewTestHarness(t)\n\tdefer h.Stop()\n\n\t// Start with fresh data directory\n\tif err := h.Start(ctx); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to start mothership: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Step 1: GET / should return the dashboard (200 OK)\n\tresp, err := http.Get(h.APIURL + \"/\")\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to GET /: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tresp.Body.Close()\n\tif resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected status 200 for GET /, got %d\", resp.StatusCode)\n\t}\n\tt.Log(\"✓ Dashboard served (200 OK)\")\n\n\t// Step 2: Check auth status - should show pin_configured: false\n\tauthStatusResp, err := http.Get(h.APIURL + \"/api/auth/status\")\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to GET /api/auth/status: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer authStatusResp.Body.Close()\n\n\tvar authStatus map[string]interface{}\n\tif err := json.NewDecoder(authStatusResp.Body).Decode(&authStatus); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to decode auth status: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tpinConfigured, _ := authStatus[\"pin_configured\"].(bool)\n\tif pinConfigured {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected pin_configured: false on fresh install\")\n\t}\n\tt.Log(\"✓ First-run setup detected (pin_configured: false)\")\n\n\t// Step 3: Complete first-run PIN setup\n\tsetupReq, _ := json.Marshal(map[string]string{\"pin\": \"0000\"})\n\tsetupResp, err := http.Post(h.APIURL+\"/api/auth/setup\", \"application/json\", bytes.NewReader(setupReq))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to POST /api/auth/setup: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer setupResp.Body.Close()\n\n\tif setupResp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {\n\t\tbody, _ := io.ReadAll(setupResp.Body)\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to complete setup: status %d: %s\", setupResp.StatusCode, string(body))\n\t}\n\tt.Log(\"✓ First-run PIN setup completed\")\n\n\t// Step 4: Verify PIN persists - check auth status again\n\tauthStatusResp2, err := http.Get(h.APIURL + \"/api/auth/status\")\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to GET /api/auth/status after setup: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer authStatusResp2.Body.Close()\n\n\tvar authStatus2 map[string]interface{}\n\tif err := json.NewDecoder(authStatusResp2.Body).Decode(&authStatus2); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to decode health: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tif health.Status != \"ok\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected health status ok, got %s\", health.Status)\n\t}\n\tt.Log(\"✓ /healthz green\")\n\n\t// Step 6: Verify no nodes attached\n\tnodes, err := h.GetNodes(ctx)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to get nodes: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tif len(nodes) != 0 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected 0 nodes on fresh install, got %d\", len(nodes))\n\t}\n\tt.Log(\"✓ Server ready with no nodes attached\")\n\n\tt.Log(\"✓ IO-1 PASSED: Fresh install / first boot\")\n}\n\n// IO_2_IdempotentRestart_Upgrade tests the idempotent restart & upgrade-in-place scenario.\n// This is a hard-gate test for releases.\n//\n// Setup: a configured install (PIN, >=1 onboarded node, zones).\n// Steps: stop + restart on the same volume.\n// Pass: no re-setup prompt; PIN/nodes/zones intact; on restart the log shows\n// \"Schema migration applied: version X -> Y\" exactly once (or skip if already on latest),\n// prior data readable, a pre-upgrade DB backup exists.\nfunc IO_2_IdempotentRestart_Upgrade(t *testing.T) {\n\tif testing.Short() {\n\t\tt.Skip(\"skipping e2e test in short mode\")\n\t}\n\n\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), TestTimeout)\n\tdefer cancel()\n\n\t// Create a temporary data directory that will persist across restarts\n\ttmpDir, err := os.MkdirTemp(\"\", \"spaxel-io2-*\")\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to create temp dir: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer os.RemoveAll(tmpDir)\n\n\t// Phase 1: Initial setup with PIN and a node\n\tt.Log(\"=== Phase 1: Initial setup ===\")\n\n\th1 := NewTestHarness(t)\n\th1.MothershipCmd = exec.CommandContext(ctx, \"/tmp/spaxel-mothership-test\")\n\th1.MothershipCmd.Env = append(os.Environ(),\n\t\t\"SPAXEL_BIND_ADDR=127.0.0.1:8080\",\n\t\t\"SPAXEL_DATA_DIR=\"+tmpDir,\n\t\t\"SPAXEL_LOG_LEVEL=info\",\n\t\t\"TZ=UTC\",\n\t)\n\th1.MothershipCmd.Stdout = io.Discard\n\th1.MothershipCmd.Stderr = io.Discard\n\n\tif err := h1.MothershipCmd.Start(); {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_51bf509fe2b64e1e815232d3","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t---\n2\tname: br-close-claimed-at-rfc3339\n3\tdescription: \"br close fails with \\\"Invalid claimed_at format: premature end of input\\\" — fix by converting worker_sessions timestamps to RFC3339\"\n4\tmetadata: \n5\t  node_type: memory\n6\t  type: reference\n7\t  originSessionId: 7f606246-9db4-4a7f-be95-650beddf4385\n8\t---\n9\t\n10\tIn the spaxel workspace, `br close <id>` fails with `Error: Invalid claimed_at format: premature end of input`. Root cause: bead-forge writes `worker_sessions.claimed_at` as a naive SQLite timestamp `'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS'` (space-separated, no timezone), but `br close` parses it strictly as RFC3339 and chokes. ALL `worker_sessions` rows had this bad format and ZERO beads had ever been closed here — so `br close` was globally broken. (It writes `closed_at` correctly as RFC3339; only the claim-write path is buggy.) bead `bf-49fp` had been auto-retried ~11,885 times because no agent could close it. Related to the bead-forge bug tracked in CLAUDE.md ([[bead-forge]] repair-fix bead bf-2yj7 family).\n11\t\n12\t**Why:** `br close` reads the most recent unclosed claim for the bead and parses `claimed_at`; one bad value aborts the whole close. Retrying the bead does not help — the failure is deterministic in the db.\n13\t\n14\t**How to apply:** When `br close` throws \"premature end of input\" / \"Invalid claimed_at format\":\n15\t1. `br sync --flush-only` — checkpoint bead content (issues/comments) to JSONL.\n16\t2. Back up the db: `cp .beads/beads.db .beads/beads.db.bak`.\n17\t3. Convert only the naive rows (leaving valid RFC3339 untouched):\n18\t   ```python\n19\t   import sqlite3, re\n20\t   con = sqlite3.connect('.beads/beads.db')\n21\t   naive = re.compile(r'^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2} \\d{2}:\\d{2}:\\d{2}$')\n22\t   rows = con.execute('SELECT rowid, claimed_at FROM worker_sessions').fetchall()\n23\t   con.execute('BEGIN')\n24\t   for rid, ts in rows:\n25\t       if naive.match(ts or ''):\n26\t           con.execute('UPDATE worker_sessions SET claimed_at=? WHERE rowid=?',\n27\t                       (ts[:10]+'T'+ts[11:]+'+00:00', rid))\n28\t   con.commit()\n29\t   ```\n30\t   (UTC confirmed safe: most recent claim's wall-clock matches `issues.updated_at` which is `+00:00`.)\n31\t4. `br close <id>` now succeeds; `br sync --flush-only`; remove the `.bak`.\n32\tNOTE: only the `worker_sessions` table (dispatch telemetry) is touched — never the `issues` table (bead content) or comments.\n33\t\n34\t**Update (2026-07-06):** the *global* break is fixed — 12269 of ~12271 `worker_sessions` rows are now valid RFC3339 and beads do close. But the claim-write bug **persists for new dispatches**: a fresh claim still writes `claimed_at` as naive `CURRENT_TIMESTAMP` (`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`, length 19), so expect ~1–2 bad rows per new dispatch — the targeted repair above is **recurring**, not one-time. Confirmed again on bf-18yn (two length-19 rows from today's dispatch blocked the close; converting them unblocked it). The `sqlite3` binary IS available via Nix at `/nix/store/*-sqlite-3.51.2-bin/bin/sqlite3` (not on PATH) — a one-liner repair works:\n35\t```bash\n36\tSQLITE=$(ls -d /nix/store/*-sqlite-*-bin/bin/sqlite3 | head -1)\n37\t$SQLITE .beads/beads.db \"UPDATE worker_sessions SET claimed_at=strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S+00:00',claimed_at) WHERE claimed_at LIKE '____-__-__ __:__:__';\"\n38\t```\n39\t(`python3 -c \"import sqlite3...\"` still works as a fallback if no Nix sqlite3 is present.)\n40\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"44dc86ef-ef62-4301-bd6e-da60373c954f","uuid":"14c89333-289b-46ac-971b-78d5becb5676","timestamp":"2026-07-07T00:46:47.708Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/.claude/projects/-home-coding-spaxel/memory/br-close-claimed-at-rfc3339.md","content":"---\nname: br-close-claimed-at-rfc3339\ndescription: \"br close fails with \\\"Invalid claimed_at format: premature end of input\\\" — fix by converting worker_sessions timestamps to RFC3339\"\nmetadata: \n  node_type: memory\n  type: reference\n  originSessionId: 7f606246-9db4-4a7f-be95-650beddf4385\n---\n\nIn the spaxel workspace, `br close <id>` fails with `Error: Invalid claimed_at format: premature end of input`. Root cause: bead-forge writes `worker_sessions.claimed_at` as a naive SQLite timestamp `'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS'` (space-separated, no timezone), but `br close` parses it strictly as RFC3339 and chokes. ALL `worker_sessions` rows had this bad format and ZERO beads had ever been closed here — so `br close` was globally broken. (It writes `closed_at` correctly as RFC3339; only the claim-write path is buggy.) bead `bf-49fp` had been auto-retried ~11,885 times because no agent could close it. Related to the bead-forge bug tracked in CLAUDE.md ([[bead-forge]] repair-fix bead bf-2yj7 family).\n\n**Why:** `br close` reads the most recent unclosed claim for the bead and parses `claimed_at`; one bad value aborts the whole close. Retrying the bead does not help — the failure is deterministic in the db.\n\n**How to apply:** When `br close` throws \"premature end of input\" / \"Invalid claimed_at format\":\n1. `br sync --flush-only` — checkpoint bead content (issues/comments) to JSONL.\n2. Back up the db: `cp .beads/beads.db .beads/beads.db.bak`.\n3. Convert only the naive rows (leaving valid RFC3339 untouched):\n   ```python\n   import sqlite3, re\n   con = sqlite3.connect('.beads/beads.db')\n   naive = re.compile(r'^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2} \\d{2}:\\d{2}:\\d{2}$')\n   rows = con.execute('SELECT rowid, claimed_at FROM worker_sessions').fetchall()\n   con.execute('BEGIN')\n   for rid, ts in rows:\n       if naive.match(ts or ''):\n           con.execute('UPDATE worker_sessions SET claimed_at=? WHERE rowid=?',\n                       (ts[:10]+'T'+ts[11:]+'+00:00', rid))\n   con.commit()\n   ```\n   (UTC confirmed safe: most recent claim's wall-clock matches `issues.updated_at` which is `+00:00`.)\n4. `br close <id>` now succeeds; `br sync --flush-only`; remove the `.bak`.\nNOTE: only the `worker_sessions` table (dispatch telemetry) is touched — never the `issues` table (bead content) or comments.\n\n**Update (2026-07-06):** the *global* break is fixed — 12269 of ~12271 `worker_sessions` rows are now valid RFC3339 and beads do close. But the claim-write bug **persists for new dispatches**: a fresh claim still writes `claimed_at` as naive `CURRENT_TIMESTAMP` (`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`, length 19), so expect ~1–2 bad rows per new dispatch — the targeted repair above is **recurring**, not one-time. Confirmed again on bf-18yn (two length-19 rows from today's dispatch blocked the close; converting them unblocked it). The `sqlite3` binary IS available via Nix at `/nix/store/*-sqlite-3.51.2-bin/bin/sqlite3` (not on PATH) — a one-liner repair works:\n```bash\nSQLITE=$(ls -d /nix/store/*-sqlite-*-bin/bin/sqlite3 | head -1)\n$SQLITE .beads/beads.db \"UPDATE worker_sessions SET claimed_at=strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S+00:00',claimed_at) WHERE claimed_at LIKE '____-__-__ __:__:__';\"\n```\n(`python3 -c \"import sqlite3...\"` still works as a fallback if no Nix sqlite3 is present.)\n","numLines":40,"startLine":1,"totalLines":40}}}
